channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: activists unveil banner linking ex-prince andrew to jeffrey epstein at buckingham palace
item.description: a group of anti-monarchy activists has unfurled a provocative banner depicting convicted pedophile jeffrey epstein and former duke of york andrew mountbatten-windsor at buckingham palace, symbolizing ...

item.title: Former ANC Mayor Joins MK Party in KwaZulu-Natal
item.description: Zandile Gumede, the former mayor of Ethekwini, has officially joined the Umkhonto weSizwe Party (MKP) and been appointed as deputy provincial convenor in KwaZulu-Natal. The announcement was made durin...

item.title: Bank of Tanzania Finalizes Crypto Regulations to Balance Innovation and Investor Protection
item.description: The Bank of Tanzania (BoT) is finalizing comprehensive regulations for cryptocurrencies and virtual assets, aiming to balance financial innovation with investor protection. Governor Emmanuel Tutuba an...

item.title: NBI Finds No Evidence of Hitman Linked to Sara Duterte in Impeachment Case
item.description: The article discusses the latest developments in the impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte. The National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) Regional Director Jeremy Lotoc stated that his team fo...

item.title: Four young drivers faced fines and licence suspensions after speeding incidents in Canberra
item.description: Four young drivers aged 17-23 have been fined and had their licences suspended after being caught speeding in Canberra during a police operation. The incidents occurred between July 9-11, 2026, with o...

item.title: Armed police arrest suspect in Ann Widdecombe's death investigation as counter-terrorism probe continues
item.description: The death of former MP Ann Widdecombe has sparked a major counter-terrorism investigation following the arrest of a suspect by armed police. New CCTV footage has emerged showing the individual being d...

item.title: Lahore court grants four-day remand extension for foreign women's assault suspects
item.description: A Lahore court has extended the physical remand of four suspects in a high-profile case involving the alleged kidnapping and rape of two foreign women. The suspects, including prime suspect Ahmad Raza...

item.title: Russia arrests anti-war critic ahead of September elections
item.description: Boris Nadezhdin, a Russian opposition figure known for his anti-war stance, was arrested by Russian police on July 13, 2026. He had previously attempted to run against President Vladimir Putin in the ...

item.title: Trump recommends Lindsey Graham's sister to complete his Senate term after his sudden death
item.description: President Donald Trump has recommended that South Carolina Governor Henry McMaster select Darline Graham Nordone, the sister of late Senator Lindsey Graham, to complete the remaining term of her broth...

item.title: Trump Admin Aims to Isolate International Criminal Court
item.description: The Trump administration is escalating its campaign to undermine the International Criminal Court (ICC), with officials exploring measures like travel bans, sanctions, and diplomatic pressure to isola...

item.title: BJP's Narottam Mishra's Transition from Censor Board Role to Ticket Denial Tears
item.description: Narottam Mishra, a six-time MLA and former home minister in Madhya Pradesh, has faced emotional turmoil after being denied a ticket for the Datia bypoll. Previously known for his strict oversight of B...

item.title: Tanzania Seizes 1.345 Billion Shillings in Gold to Combat Illegal Smuggling
item.description: Tanzanian authorities have intercepted gold valued at 1.345 billion Tanzanian shillings, which was being smuggled illegally across the western border in the Kagera region. The operation, conducted on ...

item.title: Kenyan Digital Marketer Rejects KSh 140k Offer, Demands Market Rate
item.description: Chege wa Wairimu, a digital marketing manager based in Mombasa, rejected a KSh 140,000 monthly salary offer from a Nairobi-based firm. The offer came with a hybrid work setup requiring four days in th...

item.title: Heat warnings issued for four Canadian provinces as temperatures soar
item.description: Environment Canada has issued heat warnings across four provinces—Saskatchewan, Manitoba, Ontario, and Quebec—as dangerously high temperatures and humidity are expected. The warning areas include sout...

item.title: US Reinstates Hormuz Blockade and Charges Ships 20% Toll Amid Regional Tensions
item.description: The United States has announced plans to reimpose a naval blockade on Iranian ports and charge ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz a 20% fee, as tensions escalate in the region. President Donal...

item.title: South African Traditional Leaders Urge Dialogue Over Xenophobia
item.description: A delegation of South African traditional leaders, led by King Ndamase Ndamase of the South Africa Kings Council, visited Zimbabwe to condemn xenophobic violence and advocate for peaceful resolution o...

item.title: Gab Pangilinan Commits to Honoring Gabriela Silang's Legacy in Upcoming Musical
item.description: Gab Pangilinan, a renowned Filipino actor, has expressed his deep sense of honor and responsibility in taking on the role of Gabriela Silang in an upcoming musical. The production, which aims to highl...

item.title: Ladakh to get UT-level body and hill councils for all seven districts under customised Article 371 framework
item.description: The Union Territory of Ladakh is set to establish a UT-level administrative body and hill councils for each of its seven districts, as announced by Chief Secretary Ashish Kundra. This move aims to str...

item.title: East Don Trail opens in Toronto, connecting Don River sections
item.description: A long-awaited section of Toronto’s ravine system, the East Don Trail, has officially opened to the public, allowing walkers and bikers to explore new portions of the east side of the Don River. Phase...

item.title: ADC Clarifies Court Ruling Doesn't Impact Primary Elections
item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC), led by Senator David Mark, has issued a statement clarifying that the recent Court of Appeal ruling does not influence the outcome of the party's primary electio...
